title: Dynamic Web Programming - Quiz 4 (Merged)
source: Merged from PDF and screenshot quiz sources

questions.questionMd: In a Promise chain such as `fetch(url).then(a).then(b)`, what value does function **b** receive as its argument?

options.text: The original URL string passed to `fetch()`
The value returned by function **a**
The HTTP status code of the response
The raw HTTP response object

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "The value returned by function **a**" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In REST API terminology, what is the "base URL"?

options.text: The full URL including all query parameters for a specific request
The fallback URL used when the primary endpoint is unavailable
The root address of the API server to which all endpoint paths are appended
The URL of the web page that consumes the API

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "The root address of the API server to which all endpoint paths are appended" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: To sort an array of album objects by year in ascending order, which comparator function should be passed to `.sort()`?

options.text: `function(a, b) { return b.year - a.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return a.year + b.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return a.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: A developer receives the string `{"name":"Ice Bear"}` from a server. Which function should they use to turn it into a usable JavaScript object?

options.text: `JSON.objectify()`
`JSON.parse()`
`JSON.decode()`
`JSON.stringify()`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`JSON.parse()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Compared to a custom plain-text format, what is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.text: JSON files are always smaller in size than plain-text files
JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object, eliminating manual parsing
JSON files do not require a server to be accessed
JSON is the only format supported by the `fetch()` API

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object, eliminating manual parsing" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: When loading album data from a custom plain-text file, which of the following is a disadvantage mentioned in the lecture?

options.text: Text files do not support arrays of items
Numbers must be manually converted from strings using functions like `parseInt()`
Plain-text files cannot be served over HTTP
The file cannot be loaded using the `fetch()` API

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "Numbers must be manually converted from strings using functions like `parseInt()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Why is the `fetch()` API described as asynchronous in the context of web programming?

options.text: It guarantees that the response arrives faster than synchronous requests
It blocks script execution until the full response is received
It automatically retries failed requests without any extra code
It allows the browser to continue executing other code while waiting for the network response

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "It allows the browser to continue executing other code while waiting for the network response" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: The lecture recommends implementing the **Album** class before the **App** class. What is the stated reason for this order?

options.text: The `fetch()` call can only be tested after the `Album` class exists
The `App` class depends on the `Album` class, whereas the `Album` class has no dependencies
JavaScript requires dependent classes to be declared first in the file
The `Album` class is simpler and requires fewer lines of code

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "The `App` class depends on the `Album` class, whereas the `Album` class has no dependencies" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following best describes a JavaScript Promise?

options.text: An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation
A synchronous function that immediately returns a value
A built-in timer that delays code execution by a specified amount
A special array type used to store multiple fetch results

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Why does the lecture suggest extracting the album rendering logic into a separate `_renderAlbums()` helper method?

options.text: To avoid exceeding the maximum allowed number of lines in a single method
So the same rendering code can be reused both after the initial fetch and after each sort operation
To prevent the browser from caching the album images between renders
Because the `fetch()` API requires a dedicated render callback

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "So the same rendering code can be reused both after the initial fetch and after each sort operation" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: When constructing a URL with multiple query parameters, which character is used to separate each parameter after the first one?
questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`&`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which HTTP method does a browser send automatically whenever you navigate to a URL in the address bar?

options.text: PUT
GET
DELETE
POST

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "GET" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In the discography page example, how does the `SortButton` class communicate a sort action back to the `App` class?

options.text: It dispatches a custom keyboard event that triggers the App's event listener
It calls an `onClickCallback` function passed to it during construction, supplying the sort function as an argument
It directly modifies the `albumInfo` property of the `App` instance
It fires a built-in DOM click event that the `App` class listens for on the document

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "It calls an `onClickCallback` function passed to it during construction, supplying the sort function as an argument" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which command is used to serve local files over HTTP so that `fetch()` can access them during development?

options.text: `npm start http`
`node server.js`
`python3 -m fetch.server`
`python3 -m http.server`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`python3 -m http.server`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which built-in JavaScript array method is used in the lecture to reorder album objects by year?

options.text: `.reduce()`
`.filter()`
`.sort()`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`.sort()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Why is `encodeURIComponent()` used on a user's search query before appending it to a fetch URL?

options.text: To encrypt the user's input before sending it to the server
To trim whitespace from the beginning and end of the search text
To convert the query string into a JSON-compatible format
To ensure special characters in the query string are properly percent-encoded and do not break the URL

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "To ensure special characters in the query string are properly percent-encoded and do not break the URL" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: What does the acronym JSON stand for?

options.text: JavaScript Object Notation
JavaScript Ordered Notation
Java Standard Object Notation
Java Serialized Object Notation

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "JavaScript Object Notation" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In the discography page example, how does the `SortButton` class communicate a sort action back to the `App` class?

options.text: It calls an `onClickCallback` function passed to it during construction, supplying the sort function as an argument
It dispatches a custom keyboard event that triggers the App's event listener
It directly modifies the `albumInfo` property of the `App` instance
It fires a built-in DOM click event that the `App` class listens for on the document

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "It calls an `onClickCallback` function passed to it during construction, supplying the sort function as an argument" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which built-in JavaScript array method is used in the lecture to reorder album objects by year?

options.text: `.reduce()`
`.sort()`
`.filter()`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`.sort()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the `fetch()` function in JavaScript?

options.text: To parse HTML documents and manipulate the DOM
To load resources asynchronously over a network using a Promise-based approach
To serialize JavaScript objects into JSON strings
To synchronously read files from the local file system

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "To load resources asynchronously over a network using a Promise-based approach" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In the album discography example, how are existing album images removed from the page before re-rendering a sorted list?

options.text: By setting `albumContainer.innerHTML = ''`
By iterating over child nodes and calling `remove()` on each
By calling `document.body.removeChild(albumContainer)`
By hiding the container with `albumContainer.style.display = 'none'`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "By setting `albumContainer.innerHTML = ''`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Given the JSON object `{"albums": [{"name": "Daydream", "year": 1995}]}` parsed into a variable called `data`, how would you access the year of the first album?

options.text: `data.albums.year[0]`
`data["year"]["albums"][0]`
`data[0].albums.year`
`data.albums[0].year`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`data.albums[0].year`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: To sort an array of album objects by year in ascending order, which comparator function should be passed to `.sort()`?

options.text: `function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return a.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return b.year - a.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return a.year + b.year; }`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: How does JavaScript read the current text entered by a user in an `<input type="text">` element?

options.text: By reading the `.textContent` property of the input element
By listening to the `input` event and reading `event.key`
By calling `getAttribute("text")` on the input element
By accessing the `.value` property of the input element

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "By accessing the `.value` property of the input element" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Why should `event.preventDefault()` be called inside a form's submit handler in a single-page web application?

options.text: To stop the browser from refreshing the page upon form submission
To disable the submit button after the first click
To block any fetch requests triggered inside the handler
To prevent the form's input fields from being cleared automatically

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "To stop the browser from refreshing the page upon form submission" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: When a method like `_onJsonReady` is passed as a callback to `.then()`, why must it be bound using `this._onJsonReady = this._onJsonReady.bind(this)` in the constructor?

options.text: To ensure that `this` inside the callback correctly refers to the class instance rather than being undefined or the global object
To prevent the method from being called more than once
To make the method execute synchronously instead of asynchronously
To allow the method to accept multiple parameters from the Promise chain

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "To ensure that `this` inside the callback correctly refers to the class instance rather than being undefined or the global object" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: A developer receives the string `{"name":"Ice Bear"}` from a server. Which function should they use to turn it into a usable JavaScript object?

options.text: `JSON.objectify()`
`JSON.decode()`
`JSON.parse()`
`JSON.stringify()`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`JSON.parse()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture's completed fetch example, which JavaScript expression creates a new image element equivalent to `document.createElement('img')`?

options.text: `document.newImage()`
`new Image()`
`Image.create()`
`new Img()`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`new Image()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following best describes a JavaScript Promise?

options.text: A built-in timer that delays code execution by a specified amount
A special array type used to store multiple fetch results
A synchronous function that immediately returns a value
An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `https://api.spotify.com/v1/search?type=album&q=beyonce`, what character marks the beginning of the query parameters?
questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`?`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is a common way to discover whether a product or service exposes a public REST API?

options.text: Browse the product's main website and look for a settings page
Search for `"product name" API` using a search engine
Check if the product has a mobile application available
Send an HTTP OPTIONS request directly to the product's base URL

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "Search for `"product name" API` using a search engine" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following best describes a RESTful API?

options.text: An API that only works with browser-side JavaScript and cannot be accessed from a server
An interface that exclusively uses the POST method to exchange data
A URL-based API that uses HTTP methods and sends requests to a base endpoint, returning a specified content type such as JSON
A protocol for rendering HTML pages dynamically using query strings

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "A URL-based API that uses HTTP methods and sends requests to a base endpoint, returning a specified content type such as JSON" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: How does JavaScript read the current text entered by a user in an `<input type="text">` element?

options.text: By accessing the `.value` property of the input element
By listening to the `input` event and reading `event.key`
By calling `getAttribute("text")` on the input element
By reading the `.textContent` property of the input element

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "By accessing the `.value` property of the input element" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following data types is natively preserved when a JavaScript object is serialized to JSON using `JSON.stringify()`?

options.text: Numbers
`undefined` values
Date objects
Functions

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "Numbers" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: To sort an array of album objects by year in ascending order, which comparator function should be passed to `.sort()`?

options.text: `function(a, b) { return a.year + b.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return a.year; }`
`function(a, b) { return b.year - a.year; }`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: What is the fundamental difference between a URL that points to a static file and a URL that represents a REST API endpoint?

options.text: A REST API endpoint returns HTML, while a static file URL always returns JSON
A REST API endpoint always requires POST, while static file URLs always use GET
A static file URL must include query parameters, while a REST API endpoint never does
A REST API endpoint causes the server to dynamically generate a response, whereas a static file URL causes the server to return a stored file

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "A REST API endpoint causes the server to dynamically generate a response, whereas a static file URL causes the server to return a stored file" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Given the JSON object `{"albums": [{"name": "Daydream", "year": 1995}]}` parsed into a variable called `data`, how would you access the year of the first album?

options.text: `data[0].albums.year`
`data.albums.year[0]`
`data.albums[0].year`
`data["year"]["albums"][0]`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`data.albums[0].year`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: To allow a form to submit when a user presses the Enter key, which event should be listened for on the `<form>` element?

options.text: click
submit
keydown
change

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "submit" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

options.text: `new Image()`
`new Img()`
`Image.create()`
`document.newImage()`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`new Image()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

options.text: `npm start http`
`python3 -m fetch.server`
`python3 -m http.server`
`node server.js`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`python3 -m http.server`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Why should `event.preventDefault()` be called inside a form's submit handler in a single-page web application?

options.text: To stop the browser from refreshing the page upon form submission
To prevent the form's input fields from being cleared automatically
To block any fetch requests triggered inside the handler
To disable the submit button after the first click

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "To stop the browser from refreshing the page upon form submission" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In a typical `fetch()` call chain, what does the first `.then()` callback receive as its parameter?

options.text: A JSON string representation of the resource
The final parsed data ready for use
An array of all fetched resources
A Response object from the network request

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "A Response object from the network request" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Why is the `fetch()` API described as asynchronous in the context of web programming?

options.text: It blocks script execution until the full response is received
It automatically retries failed requests without any extra code
It allows the browser to continue executing other code while waiting for the network response
It guarantees that the response arrives faster than synchronous requests

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "It allows the browser to continue executing other code while waiting for the network response" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `https://api.spotify.com/v1/search?type=album&q=beyonce`, what character marks the beginning of the query parameters?
questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`?`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: By default, without any additional options, which HTTP method does the `fetch()` API use when making a request?

options.text: POST
GET
DELETE
PUT

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "GET" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, which company originally developed and used GraphQL before open-sourcing it?

options.text: GitHub
Netflix
Facebook
Spotify

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "Facebook" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what does MIME type (content type) specify in the context of a RESTful API request?

options.text: The maximum size of the response body allowed
The HTTP method that must be used for the request
The authentication scheme required to access the endpoint
The format of the data being sent or returned, such as JSON, HTML, or plain text

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "The format of the data being sent or returned, such as JSON, HTML, or plain text" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: How does the lecture characterize Netflix's Falcor compared to GraphQL as an alternative to REST?

options.text: Falcor is described as the most widely adopted REST alternative in the industry
Falcor is described as a synchronous alternative to REST that avoids HTTP entirely
Falcor is described as likely interesting but rarely used in practice and lacking a Wikipedia page
Falcor is described as the predecessor to GraphQL that Facebook later adopted

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "Falcor is described as likely interesting but rarely used in practice and lacking a Wikipedia page" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In HTTP communication, what does a status code in the 200 range generally indicate?

options.text: The client must authenticate before proceeding
The server encountered an internal error
The request was successful
The resource was not found

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "The request was successful" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, which HTTP method is used specifically to send or submit data to a server?

options.text: GET
HEAD
PATCH
POST

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "POST" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which function would you use to convert a JSON string back into a JavaScript object?

options.text: `JSON.decode()`
`JSON.stringify()`
`JSON.parse()`
`JSON.hydrate()`

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`JSON.parse()`" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what does it mean to "serialize" an object?

options.text: To turn an object into a string that can later be deserialized
To load an object from an external file using fetch
To send an object directly to a server over HTTP
To convert a string into a JavaScript object

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "To turn an object into a string that can later be deserialized" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: In the lecture's chained fetch example, what is the correct order of asynchronous operations?

options.text: `fetch` completes, then `onResponse` runs, then `response.text()` completes, then `onStreamProcessed` runs
`onStreamProcessed` runs first, then `fetch` completes, then `onResponse` runs
`fetch` and `response.text()` run simultaneously before any handlers fire
`response.text()` runs before `fetch` completes

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "`fetch` completes, then `onResponse` runs, then `response.text()` completes, then `onStreamProcessed` runs" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: A Promise has a `.then()` method that accepts which of the following?

options.text: A timeout value and a retry count
A single callback that handles both success and failure identically
Functions to execute on success or on error
A URL string and an HTTP method

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "Functions to execute on success or on error" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: How does `response.text()` read the response data according to the lecture?

options.text: Synchronously, by returning the string directly
Asynchronously, by returning a Promise that resolves with the response string
By dispatching a DOM event when reading is complete
By writing the result into a global variable

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "Asynchronously, by returning a Promise that resolves with the response string" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following best describes the "pending" state of a Promise?

options.text: The state where the error handler has been triggered
The state where the operation completed but returned no data
The state where `.then()` has been called but not yet attached
The initial state where the operation has not yet fulfilled or rejected

questions.explanationMd: The correct answer is "The initial state where the operation has not yet fulfilled or rejected" based on the lecture/examples in the provided quiz sources.
